titleEn: Seminar “Science, technology and innovation in Brazil: the role and challenges of public universities”
excerptEn: From 27 to 31 March the Centre for Brazilian Studies of the USAL is organising the seminar “Science, technology and innovation in Brazil: the role and challenges of public universities”, which will be taught by Professor Carlos Alexandre Netto, of the Federal University of Rio Grande do Sul. The seminar is free of charge and is…
contentEn:
From 27 to 31 March the Centre for Brazilian Studies of the USAL is organising the seminar “Science, technology and innovation in Brazil: the role and challenges of public universities”, which will be taught by Professor Carlos Alexandre Netto, of the Federal University of Rio Grande do Sul.

The seminar is free of charge and is aimed at postgraduate students (Master's and doctoral) and teaching and research staff.

Full details are also available here: http://www.cebusal.es/seminario-ciencia-tecnologia-e-innovacion-en-brasil/

titleEn: 9th Meeting of the heads of Teaching Training/Innovation of the Public Universities of Castilla y León
excerptEn: On 2 March the 9th Meeting of the heads of Teaching Training/Innovation was held, with the Universities of Burgos (Javier Hoyuelos, IFIE) and León (José Carlos Pena and Miriam, Training School), Alfredo Corell (Academic Area for Training and Innovation) and María José Rodríguez Conde (IUCE). The meeting focused above all…
contentEn:
On 2 March the 9th Meeting of the heads of Teaching Training/Innovation was held, with the Universities of Burgos (Javier Hoyuelos, IFIE) and León (José Carlos Pena and Miriam, Training School), Alfredo Corell (Academic Area for Training and Innovation) and María José Rodríguez Conde (IUCE).

The meeting focused above all on the design of the forthcoming 1st International Teaching Innovation Conference, with the support of the Directorate for Universities of the Junta de Castilla y León, as well as on the preliminary analysis for the proposal to ACSUCYL of a draft «Quality/accreditation model for the Teaching Training Programmes/Plans for University Teaching Staff in Castilla y León».

The director of GRIAL, Francisco J. García Peñalvo, dropped by to greet the working team.

titleEn: Visit by professors from the Freie Universität Berlin
excerptEn: On Friday 24 February the coordinator of the “Education in the Knowledge Society” doctoral programme, Francisco J. García Peñalvo, and the director of the IUCE, María José Rodríguez Conde, received a visit from Doctors Margarita Esponda-Arguero (www.esponda.de) and Raúl Rojas (http://dcis.inf.fu-berlin.de/home/workgroups/raul-rojas-intelligent-system-and-robotics/), professors at the…
contentEn:
On Friday 24 February the coordinator of the “Education in the Knowledge Society” doctoral programme, Francisco J. García Peñalvo, and the director of the IUCE, María José Rodríguez Conde, received a visit from Doctors Margarita Esponda-Arguero (www.esponda.de) and Raúl Rojas (http://dcis.inf.fu-berlin.de/home/workgroups/raul-rojas-intelligent-system-and-robotics/), professors at the Institute of Computer Science of the Freie Universität Berlin.

The visit was coordinated by Professor Erla Morales Morgado, with the aim of establishing cooperation links between the two institutions, begun last January following her visit to the Freie Universität.

These initiatives include putting into practice at the University of Salamanca the INTELLICHALK tool, developed in Germany by a doctoral student of Professor Esponda, whose pedagogical aspects are being studied in the thesis of Claudia Muñoz Jara, a researcher on the Education in the Knowledge Society doctoral programme.

excerptEn: On 1 and 2 June 2017 the University of Alicante will host REDES-INNOVAESTIC 2017, which brings together the “15th Conference on Research Networks in University Teaching” and the “1st International Workshop on Innovation in Higher Education and ICT”. Some noteworthy aspects of REDES-INNOVAESTIC 2017 are: The 15th Conference and the…
contentEn:
On 1 and 2 June 2017 the University of Alicante will host REDES-INNOVAESTIC 2017, which brings together the “15th Conference on Research Networks in University Teaching” and the “1st International Workshop on Innovation in Higher Education and ICT”.

Some noteworthy aspects of REDES-INNOVAESTIC 2017 are:

  • The 15th Conference and the 1st International Workshop will be held jointly, so the ICE will issue participants with a joint certificate referring explicitly to both activities.
  • Authors' proposals for oral papers and posters will be based on an abstract (between 500 and 600 words). It is therefore not compulsory to submit the full text in order to take part in the paper or poster sessions.
  • Three volumes will be published:
    • Editorial Octaedro (Q1 in the Scholarly Publishers Indicators-CSIC) will publish the volume entitled Research in university teaching. Designing the future through educational innovation (digital format, with ISBN), endowed with an International Scientific Committee. It will include full texts accepted after the double blind peer review process.
    • In addition, the ICE of the University of Alicante will publish the Book of Proceedings (in digital format) based on the abstracts of the accepted proposals for oral papers and posters, and a further volume (digital format, with ISBN), endowed with an International Scientific Committee. It will include full texts accepted after the double blind peer review process.
  • The deadline for submitting proposals (abstract) is 22 March.

Further information on the website: https://web.ua.es/es/ice/redes-innovaestic2017

titleEn: Call for contributions open for the ‘A Million Pictures’ conference
excerptEn: A Million Pictures: History, Archiving, and Creative Re-use of Educational Magic Lantern Slides The magic lantern was an important visual entertainment and means of instruction in nineteenth-century Europe and its former colonies; it was used in popular and academic teaching until the 1950s. However, despite its pervasiveness across multiple scientific, educational and popular contexts, magic…
contentEn:
A Million Pictures: History, Archiving, and Creative Re-use of Educational Magic Lantern Slides

The magic lantern was an important visual entertainment and means of instruction in nineteenth-century Europe and its former colonies; it was used in popular and academic teaching until the 1950s. However, despite its pervasiveness across multiple scientific, educational and popular contexts, magic lantern slides remain under-researched.
The project and the conference engage with magic lantern slides on several levels:

  • studying the historical importance of magic lantern slides, projection apparatus, readings, and other paraphernalia as tools in the history of learning in a wide range of fields
  • exchanging archival practices and discussing tools for cataloguing, archiving and giving access to magic lantern slides held in heritage institutions and other collections
  • stimulating the re-use of this form of cultural heritage in scholarly, artistic, documentary, cultural and other creative practices.

The conference will present and discuss the outcomes of the Million Pictures project and share them with all those interested in the magic lantern and lantern slides from a scholarly, historical, archival, curatorial, artistic, museological, educational or practical point of view. It will also provide a platform for both presenting ongoing scholarly research on the magic lantern, with an emphasis on lantern slides as a teaching tool as well as for presenting activities in the fields of archiving and creative re-use.

A keynote will be given by media historian Prof. Erkki Huhtamo (UCLA – Design Media Arts / Film, Television, and Digital Media) and a closing statement by Prof. Vanessa Toulmin (University of Sheffield / Research Director of the National Fairground Archive). The programme will also contain magic lantern performances, roundtables and demonstrations.

The conference invites scholars, practitioners in the heritage sector, in the creative industries, in the arts and in cultural/museum education to participate and submit a contribution.

We invite contributions in the following formats:

Scholarly Papers
We invite scholarly papers on the magic lantern and educational or non-fictional magic lantern slides that have a connection to the broad history of learning. These sessions will present ongoing or recently completed research by scholars from different fields such as Media History, Theatre and Performance Studies, History of Science, Cultural History, Word and Image Studies, Geography, and other disciplinary areas relevant to the lantern and slides. Presentations should not exceed 20 minutes and will be followed by discussions.
Possible submissions can address (but are not limited to):

  • Lantern slides and their role in various disciplinary histories
  • Debates on teaching with lantern slides
  • Performativity of lantern shows in scientific and other contexts
  • Lantern slide lectures in local science, geography, natural history and other clubs and societies
  • Science popularisers and the magic lantern
  • Lantern use in regional, national or transnational contexts
  • Production and distribution of lantern slides
  • Circuits and tours of lantern slide lecturers and other performers
  • Education of children and/or adults

Poster Presentations
We invite poster presentations on the wider topic of lantern slides in current research projects, (museological) education and established methods of giving access to this form of cultural heritage (digitization, documentation, public engagement) as well as wider aspects of lantern culture.

Demonstrations and Presentations of Artistic Forms of Engaging with Lantern Slides
We invite practitioners and performers to contribute documentations of artistic performances, to present film material, demonstrate a (digital) tool, or prepare another form of encounter or discussion. Please specify your topic/project and the form of presentation in your submission as well as specific requirements (room, technology, maximum number of participants). Contributions in either format do not have a minimum amount of time, but should not be longer than 20-30 minutes.

Input to Roundtable Discussions
We plan two roundtable discussions dedicated to the topics “Public activities with the Magic Lantern in archives and museums” and “Creative re-use of Magic Lantern slides”. We invite people with experience in these fields to give input to these roundtables.

5 Minutes of Experience Talks
Curators, collectors, practitioners, but also scholars are invited to prepare a five-minute presentation on their experiences obtained in or questions raised by a project concerning magic lantern slides which they would like to discuss with the specialists present during the conference. The 5 Minutes of Experience Talks will provide input for further discussion and should address the following questions: what was the aim of your project? How did you go about it? What was the result? What did you learn? What would you recommend?

Proposals for contributions in all forms should not exceed 5.000 characters and should be accompanied by a short bio (max. 1.000 characters).

Deadline for submissions is 31 January 2017.

Proposals for contributions should be submitted via the submission form:
https://fd8.formdesk.com/universiteitutrecht/a-million-pictures

Acceptance will be announced in March 2017.

We are aware that not all potential participants are working at an institution that can cover their expenses. At the moment, we are applying for additional funding to offer fee waivers and, hopefully, part of the expenses. Conference fees, including lunches and refreshments, will not exceed 20-25 Euro per day.

For questions, please contact Prof. Frank E. Kessler (f.e.kessler @ uu.nl ) or Dr. Sarah Dellmann (s.dellmann @ uu.nl).

Download the call here.

titleEn: SNOLA in the report «Research Evidence on the Use of Learning Analytics: Implications for Education Policy»
excerptEn: The report “Research Evidence on the Use of Learning Analytics: Implications for Education Policy”, published by the European Commission's Science and Knowledge Service in 2016, recognises the SNOLA network (Spanish Network of Learning Analytics) as a relevant actor in the field of Learning Analytics within the European Union. The SNOLA…
contentEn:
The report “Research Evidence on the Use of Learning Analytics: Implications for Education Policy”, published by the European Commission's Science and Knowledge Service in 2016, recognises the SNOLA network (Spanish Network of Learning Analytics) as a relevant actor in the field of Learning Analytics within the European Union. The SNOLA Research Network is a Network of Excellence currently recognised and funded by the Ministry of Economy and Competitiveness, and the GRIAL group is one of its members.
The report can be accessed here (https://ec.europa.eu/jrc/en/publication/eur-scientific-and-technical-research-reports/research-evidence-use-learning-analytics-implications-education-policy) or via its DOI http://dx.doi.org/10.2791/955210

titleEn: 14th International Congress on Theory of Education: “Education facing the challenges of a new citizenship”
excerptEn: The 14th International Congress on Theory of Education: “Education facing the challenges of a new citizenship”, organised by the Department of Theory and History of Education of the University of Murcia and the academic network Interuniversity Seminar on Theory of Education (SITE), will be held at the University of Murcia on…
contentEn:
The 14th International Congress on Theory of Education: “Education facing the challenges of a new citizenship”, organised by the Department of Theory and History of Education of the University of Murcia and the academic network Interuniversity Seminar on Theory of Education (SITE), will be held at the University of Murcia from 21 to 23 November 2017

The 14th Congress, international in scope and with a clear interdisciplinary vocation, is conceived as a space for debate, reflection and exchange on the capacity of citizenship and education to respond to the imminent challenges posed by today's society.

The Congress has three main thematic strands:

1. Ideological, political, cultural and religious perspectives on the condition of citizenship.

2. Educational implications of the Third Sector in the construction of citizenship.

3. The role of education in the construction of citizenship.

We encourage education professionals and all those interested in these questions to take part in this gathering with their attendance and contributions, because citizenship is everyone's business.

For further information: http://cite2017.com/

titleEn: CFP: ISATT 2017 – 18th Biennial Conference on Teachers and Teaching
excerptEn: ISATT 2017 18th BIENNIAL CONFERENCE ON TEACHERS AND TEACHING 3-7 JULY 2017 UNIVERSITY OF SALAMANCA, SALAMANCA, SPAIN “Teaching search and research” CALL FOR ABSTRACTS Dear researcher, You are invited to join the 18th Biennial Conference on Teachers and Teaching – ISATT 2017, one of the largest international education conferences for teachers, lectures and educational…
contentEn:
ISATT 2017

18th BIENNIAL CONFERENCE ON TEACHERS AND TEACHING

3-7 JULY 2017

UNIVERSITY OF SALAMANCA, SALAMANCA, SPAIN

“Teaching search and research”

 

CALL FOR ABSTRACTS

Dear researcher,

You are invited to join the 18th Biennial Conference on Teachers and Teaching – ISATT 2017, one of the largest international education conferences for teachers, lectures and educational researchers.

CONFERENCE THEME

We invite candidates to contribute to the conference theme: “Teaching search and research”. The essence of this conference aims to capture the mutual interrelations between the academia and schools in order to combine discourses and align positions. The particular interest is to bring practice into theory and theory to practice. Education needs a genuine understanding between these two worlds in order to offer adapted responses to multiple problems and increasingly diverse contexts.

Therefore the ISATT 2017 will encourage submissions that examine the diverse teaching contexts and the many changes occurring across education research and practice: from design to implementation. All areas where learning and pedagogy have decisive presence are susceptible to be included. ISATT 2017 is conceived as an open forum to share research works and teaching experiences in early childhood, elementary and secondary school education as well as tertiary education.

SUB-THEMES

The scope of the conference includes the following sub-themes:

  • The development of knowledge and understanding of teaching practices.
  • Social competencies and problem-solving competencies.
  • Engaging learning environments
  • Sustainability of learning and teaching practices.

Innovative practices and ICT (i.e., virtual platforms, mobile & augmented learning, flipped learning, etc.)

PRESENTATION FORMATS

You are invited to submit your contributions in English in any of the following formats:

  • Oral Presentations
  • Symposia
  • Workshops

You are also invited to submit POSTERS in Spanish or Portuguese.

You can find further information here

IMPORTANT DATES

Call for abstracts: 3 October 2016

Registration opens: 31 October 2016

Abstract submission due: 28 December 2016

Authors notified: 28 February 2017

TOP 5 REASONS TO ATTEND ISATT 2017

  • Present your contribution in one of the largest international conference on education in the world.
  • Contribute to bring practice into theory and theory to practice and contribute to bring together both “search” and “research” with the purpose of offering solutions to real problems in the educational field.
  • Participate in this open forum to share research works and teaching experiences in early childhood, elementary and secondary school education as well as tertiary education.
  • Learn about educational innovations to improve your educational practices.
  • Interact with international teachers, lecturers and educators from all over the world.

PUBLICATIONS

All contributions will be published in the conference proceedings. In addition, selected research works will be invited for inclusion in a book published by SENSE PUBLISHERS and also in special issues of the following journals:

  • Revista Curriculum y Formación del Profesorado. Indexed in SCOPUS and the Emerging Sources Citation Index (ESCI). Web of Science. Thomson Reuters.
  • Journal of Information Technology Research (JITR). Indexed in SCOPUS.
  • RELATEC journal. Indexed in the Emerging Sources Citation Index (ESCI). Web of Science. Thomson Reuters.
  • Education in the knowledge society. Indexed in Latindex, DICE and Directory of Open Access Journals (DOAJ).
  • Enseñanza & Teaching. Indexed in Latindex, DICE and Directory of Open Access Journal (DOAJ).

Submitted manuscripts will be subjected to the journals’ peer review processes and editorial policies.

ABOUT SALAMANCA AND THE UNIVERSITY OF SALAMANCA

The city of Salamanca is a beautiful city in northwestern Spain, situated approximately 200 kilometres (120 miles) west of the Spanish capital Madrid. Its Old City was declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1988. Also, it is a city to enjoy and visit, with a lot of monuments to sightsee, for instance the Plaza Mayor, the Huerto de Calisto y Melibea and the Cathedrals. Furthermore, there is a high speed train from Madrid to Salamanca (duration: one and a half hours).

In terms of conferences, Salamanca is a usual scenario for international conferences in many disciplines. In fact, the University of Salamanca (USAL) is the oldest University in Spain (and fourth oldest in Europe). It is a medium size university with an average of more than 30,000 registered students each year and made up of nine teaching campuses, a Science Park and 12 research institutes.

Come and join us! We are looking forward to seeing you in the Conference!

Please visit http://isatt2017.com/ for more information

The CfP is also available to download here.

titleEn: The IUCE and CITEC-B win awards once again at VIDEOMED 2016
excerptEn: The University Institute for Research in Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca, together with the Centre for Imaging and Biomedical Knowledge Technology of Madrid (CITEC-B), with which the Institute holds a collaboration agreement through the VisualMed System research group, have been honoured with two awards at…
contentEn:
The University Institute for Research in Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca, together with the Centre for Imaging and Biomedical Knowledge Technology of Madrid (CITEC-B), with which the Institute holds a collaboration agreement through the VisualMed System research group, have been honoured with two awards at the XX International Festival of Health and Medical films and Telemedicine (VIDEOMED 2016), held in Badajoz from 21 to 26 November 2016.

The works were presented by Professor Dr Juan A. Juanes Méndez, of the University of Salamanca, deputy director of the Institute and head of the research group on Advanced Medical Visualisation Systems (VisualMed System). In his presentation at Videomed16, Professor Juanes noted “that these ways of analysing and understanding the different pathologies optimise learning outcomes by providing the user with a good environment for visualising the medical content to be conveyed, giving them a high degree of assimilation of concepts”. Dr Juan A. Juanes also pointed out that these new visual technological methods make it possible to approach and study different pathologies in a more intuitive and didactic way, which will undoubtedly lead to better medical training.

The award-winning titles at Videomed 2016 were: Huntington's disease and Ankylosing spondylitis

The film on Huntington's disease is a rich source of information that makes it possible to create medical training strategies for the study and analysis of this brain pathology. It is diagnosed by means of genetic and neurological tests. In this pathology, problems may appear in the following categories: motor control (movement), cognition (reasoning) and behaviour.

The film on ankylosing spondylitis shows the clinical features of this chronic inflammatory disease, which mainly affects the joints of the spine. It is so named because it causes the vertebrae to fuse together, resulting in restricted mobility.

The audiovisual materials presented make it possible to convey, in an appropriate manner, the information needed to achieve optimum levels of effectiveness and efficiency in the training of medical students on these pathologies, equipping them with the skills required for diagnosis.

When presenting these materials, Dr Juanes noted that “the application of these didactic audiovisual procedures in medical training provides additional support to face-to-face teaching, with the added advantages of flexible timing and learning at a personalised pace”.

There is no doubt that medical training, supported by audiovisual technological methods such as those presented at Videomed2016, fosters the teaching-learning process in the Health Sciences.

titleEn: The IUCE hosted the fourth edition of the TEEM'16 conference
excerptEn: In early November — specifically from 2 to 4 November 2016 — the fourth edition of the international conference Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality (TEEM'16) took place. The conference was attended by more than 200 participants from 32 different countries, organised into 16 sessions with a common thread: fostering multiculturality from…
contentEn:
In early November — specifically from 2 to 4 November 2016 — the fourth edition of the international conference Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality (TEEM’16) took place. The conference was attended by more than 200 participants from 32 different countries, organised into 16 sessions with a common thread: fostering multiculturality from the interdisciplinary perspective characteristic of the Knowledge Society.

The opening ceremony took place in the Assembly Hall of the Faculty of Education of the University of Salamanca and was attended by the Vice-Rector for Research and Transfer, Juan Manuel Corchado; the Director-General for Universities and Research, Pilar Garcés García; the director of the University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca, Dr María José Rodríguez Conde; and the chair of the conference, Francisco José García Peñalvo.

All the presentations from the event will be available in the coming days on SlideShare http://slideshare.net/teemconference. Likewise, the sessions streamed live can be viewed on Periscope https://www.periscope.tv/teemconference and will be available on the conference's YouTube channel https://youtube.com/teemconference.

titleEn: Call for the 1st International Congress on Social and Productive Innovation
excerptEn: The Vice-Rectorate for Research of the Pedro Ruiz Gallo National University of Lambayeque, Peru, with the sponsorship of the Interaction and eLearning Research Group (Grial) of the University of Salamanca, Spain, and the Latin American Academic Network Diálogos en Mercosur of Brazil, is issuing the CALL – 2016 for the 1st International Congress on Social and…
contentEn:
The Vice-Rectorate for Research of the Pedro Ruiz Gallo National University of Lambayeque, Peru, with the sponsorship of the Interaction and eLearning Research Group (Grial) of the University of Salamanca, Spain, and the Latin American Academic Network Diálogos en Mercosur of Brazil, is issuing the CALL – 2016 for the 1st International Congress on Social and Productive Innovation (ICIISP), which will take place from 12 to 15 April 2017 in Lambayeque, Peru.

The ICIISP is conceived as a space for interaction, promotion, development and evaluation of the impact of scientific and technological knowledge on society, the environment and economies, in order to attract interesting contributions at national and world level, through specialist keynote lectures, research papers, publication in a scientific journal and co-authorship of the book of the New Network.

OBJECTIVES

  1. To disseminate the progress and results of the scientific and technological research work of teachers, students and private researchers, at local, national and international level.
  2. To contribute to the development of research lines on social and productive innovation.
  3. To create the Scientific and Technological Network for Social and Productive Innovation.

THEMATIC AREAS

A) Social innovation

A.1. Capacities and entrepreneurship for social innovation
A.2. Innovation in the field of health and education
A.3. Innovation for personal, family, workplace and community well-being
A.4. Innovation for the protection, improvement and conservation of the environment
A.5. Innovation for land-use planning and sustainable urban development
A.6. Evaluation and promotion of social innovation

B) Productive innovation

B.1. Capacities and entrepreneurship for productive innovation
B.2. Technological research for local development
B.3. Creativity, technological development and intellectual protection
B.4. Innovative national and international business and innovation in business
B.5. Tourism (innovative, nature, cultural, rural, community, sustainable, gastronomic and agro-ecotourism)
B.6. Evaluation and promotion of productive innovation

SUBMISSION OF WORKS

  • Article or poster abstract
  • Scientific article or poster
  • Paper

SELECTION CRITERIA

  1. The topic must fall within one of the areas of the Congress
  2. Originality in the approach to the topic
  3. Quality of argumentation and grounding
  4. Relevance to human, institutional, local and regional development

TIMETABLE
Call: August – December 2016
Receipt of abstracts: Until 26 November 2016
Receipt of scientific articles and posters: Until 31 December 2016
Delivery of acceptance letters: 15 February 2017
Publication of the programme of papers and posters: From 1 March 2017
Presentation of lectures, articles and posters: 12 – 15 April

ABSTRACT SUBMISSION
Those interested should send their abstract to angelicadcr@hotmail.com; from there, depending on the thematic area, it will be forwarded to the Area Coordinator for review and follow-up.

Abstracts should be submitted in Times New Roman, 12, single-spaced. They must contain:

  • Title of the article, centred and in bold, font size 12
  • Full first names and surnames of the author, centred below the title, font size 12
  • Name of the institution, centred, below the names and surnames, in font size 10
  • Address of the institution, centred, below the name of the institution, in font size 10
  • Telephone number, centred, below the address of the institution, in font size 10
  • Email address, centred, below the telephone number, in size 12
  • Place the thematic area on the right, in capital letters
  • The word “Abstract” to the left of the text
  • Write, in Spanish only and in no more than 150 words, the main objective and scope of the research, the methods, the results and the main conclusions.
  • Keywords.

PARTICIPATION FEES
Registration for presenters:
Until 31 December 2016: US$200; After 31 December 2016: US$250

Registration for attendees:
Until 31 December 2016: US$250; After 31 December 2016: US$300

GENERAL COORDINATOR AND CONTACT
Dr Hilda Angélica Del Carpio Ramos
angelicadcr@hotmail.com
Mobile +51 979176434; Rpm #851128; Rpc +51 949124346

You can download the CFP in PDF format here.

titleEn: Final session of the Researchers' School at the USAL
excerptEn: On Tuesday 24 May 2016, the Sala Menor of the Hospedería de Fonseca of the University of Salamanca hosted the final face-to-face session of the Researchers' School (third edition) of the Junta de Castilla y León, organised by the Junta de Castilla y León's Higher Centre for Teacher Training in…
contentEn:
On Tuesday 24 May 2016, the Sala Menor of the Hospedería de Fonseca of the University of Salamanca hosted the final face-to-face session of the Researchers' School (third edition) of the Junta de Castilla y León, organised by the Junta de Castilla y León's Higher Centre for Teacher Training in Burgos. A joint working day with groups coordinated from the University of León (Mercedes López) and the University of Valladolid (Bartolomé Rubia). More than 40 lecturer-researchers took part, belonging to three Researchers' Schools located in the four public universities of the region, the University of León, the University of Salamanca and the University of Valladolid.

María José Rodríguez Conde, Esperanza Herrera, Susana Olmos, Eva M. Torrecilla, Fernando Martínez Abad and Juan Pablo Hernández Ramos, researchers of the Educational Assessment and Guidance subgroup (GE2O) of the GRIAL research group, attended the session as coordinators of five of the projects presented.

titleEn: Meeting of the heads of Teacher Training of the UBU, ULE, USAL and UVA with the Director-General for Universities and Research at the Department of Education of the JCYL
excerptEn: On 28 April 2016 a meeting of the heads of University Teaching Training and Innovation of the four public universities of CyL was held with Pilar Garcés García, within the collaboration agreement that the four universities have maintained since 2010. The attendees were: for the UBU, the director…
contentEn: On 28 April 2016 a meeting of the heads of University Teaching Training and Innovation of the four public universities of CyL was held with Pilar Garcés García, within the collaboration agreement that the four universities have maintained since 2010. The attendees were: for the UBU, the director of the Institute for Training and Innovation (IFIE), Javier Hoyuelos; for the ULE, the director of the Training School, José Carlos Pena; for the USAL, the director of the IUCE, María José Rodríguez Conde; and for the UVA, the director of the Area for Training and Innovation, Alfredo Corell.

titleEn: Visit by students from the Escola Superior de Educação de Viseu to the IUCE
excerptEn: On Wednesday 20 April 2016 we received a visit at the IUCE from first-year students of Visual Arts and Multimedia and of Cultural Animation. They were accompanied by the lecturers Cristina Azevedo Gomes and Dalila Rodrigues and were welcomed by the director of the IUCE, María José Rodríguez Conde, and by the…
contentEn:
On Wednesday 20 April 2016 we received a visit at the IUCE from first-year students of Visual Arts and Multimedia and of Cultural Animation. They were accompanied by the lecturers Cristina Azevedo Gomes and Dalila Rodrigues and were welcomed by the director of the IUCE, María José Rodríguez Conde, and by the GRIAL researcher Juan Cruz Benito.

During their stay they were introduced to the IUCE as an interdisciplinary research institute, and in particular to the «Education in the Knowledge Society» Doctoral Programme. The research lines of GRIAL were also explained, together with the latest projects relating to Art, Technology and the Digital Humanities.

titleEn: The ULE hosts the 8th Meeting of heads of university teaching staff training in the region
excerptEn: On 11 March, the University of León hosted the 8th Meeting of heads of university teaching staff training, chaired by the Rector's delegate for Quality and Training of the ULE, José Carlos Pena Álvarez, accompanied by the coordinator of the ULE Training School, Miriam Fernández Álvarez, and…
contentEn:
On 11 March, the University of León hosted the 8th Meeting of heads of university teaching staff training chaired by the Rector's delegate for Quality and Training of the ULE, José Carlos Pena Álvarez, accompanied by the coordinator of the ULE Training School, Miriam Fernández Álvarez, and by Javier Hoyuelos Álvaro (University of Burgos), Mª José Rodríguez Conde (University of Salamanca), and Alfredo Corell Almuzara (University of Valladolid).

The meeting served to review all the inter-university activities carried out during the 2014-15 academic year, and also to analyse the proposals for courses and other training initiatives for the 2015-16 year, which will complement those already under way.

titleEn: 2nd National Congress on Educational Innovation and Online Teaching (INRED 2016)
excerptEn: The 2nd National Congress on Educational Innovation and Online Teaching (INRED 2016), organised by the Polytechnic University of Valencia on 7 and 8 July, aims to disseminate experiences and to foster debate and the exchange of ideas in order to support teaching actions carried out to improve learning that involve…
contentEn:
The 2nd National Congress on Educational Innovation and Online Teaching (INRED 2016), organised by the Polytechnic University of Valencia on 7 and 8 July, aims to disseminate experiences and to foster debate and the exchange of ideas in order to support teaching actions carried out to improve learning that involve methodological innovation as well as the development and/or use of technologies as teaching resources.

On 5 April the submission period for this event comes to an end. You can find all the information on the congress blog: http://inred.blogs.upv.es/call-for-papers/

It should also be noted that an agreement has been reached with the journal IEEE-RITA to recommend the best works presented at INRED for publication in it (http://rita.det.uvigo.es/). The recommended works must be expanded with at least 30% new material and submitted for review in Spanish. Once accepted, the authors will translate them into English for their final publication.

You can receive all the information about INRED 2016 and keep up to date with the latest news on educational innovation and online teaching on its Twitter account @InRedCongreso.
